sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, has been experiencing back pain for an extended period, with the pain becoming more severe over the past month.
He reports that the pain radiates from his lower back down through his hip and into his left leg, with the pain in the leg being described as a burning sensation, like an electrical toothache.
He rates his pain as a 6 out of 10, but when it gets worse, it is a 13 out of 10.
He mentions that standing, walking, and activities such as coughing and sneezing exacerbate the pain, while lying down provides some relief.
He has been bedridden for the past month due to the severity of his symptoms.
The patient also reports weakness in both his back and legs, with his legs being particularly weak.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vitals are good.
Physical exam: On examination, the patient is positive for pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and he experiences pain with flexion and extension of his back.
He has 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae associated with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement causing lower back pain and leg pain.
Problem list: Disk herniation at L4-L5; Nerve root impingement; Lower back pain; Leg pain; Weakness in both back and legs.
Plan:
I prescribed an epidural steroid injection to be administered today for the acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
I want the patient to see me again in about a month to reassess the effectiveness of the injection.
Follow-up: The patient will see me again in about a month to reassess the effectiveness of the epidural steroid injection.
